critic_raw:
## ML Critique (Run 3): Sequence vs. Tabular Models for SaaS Churn Prediction

**Issue 1 — Random account-level split across 36 months creates temporal leakage**
Stratified random sampling distributes accounts from any subscription cohort across all splits. Accounts created in month 34–36 can be in training; accounts from month 1–3 can be in test. SaaS product feature sets evolve, pricing changes, and customer expectations shift. Training on recently-created accounts and evaluating on older cohorts is not a valid prospective evaluation of churn prediction for new customers.

**Issue 2 — Preprocessing fit on complete modeling cohort before split**
"Preprocessing transformers are defined once from the complete modeling cohort" — standardization moments, categorical vocabularies, and imputation values all incorporate test-set account distributions. Preprocessing must be fit on training accounts only.

**Issue 3 — Baseline logistic regression uses "canonical" L2 (not tuned); LSTM/Transformer is end-to-end trained**
The baseline uses "L2 regularization held at a canonical setting rather than exhaustively tuned." The proposed LSTM/Transformer is trained end-to-end. This is an asymmetric comparison: the baseline may be suboptimally regularized while the sequence model benefits from full optimization. Any observed gap may reflect regularization quality, not temporal sequence modeling value.

**Issue 4 — ROC-AUC primary metric misaligned with B2B churn intervention objective**
B2B SaaS churn intervention operates under account capacity constraints — customer success managers can proactively outreach a limited number of accounts per period. ROC-AUC measures global discrimination, not precision at the operationally relevant threshold. AUPRC or Precision@10% (which is listed as a secondary metric) directly captures performance in the top of the risk distribution where interventions are prioritized.
defender_raw:
## Design Defense (Run 3): SaaS Churn Prediction

**Issue 1:** Random account split with plan-tier and company-size stratification addresses segment balance, not temporal direction. Accounts from month 34–36 in training, month 1–3 in test = temporal inversion for prospective prediction. Conceded.

**Issue 2:** "Complete modeling cohort" preprocessing = test-account distribution informs features. Conceded.

**Issue 3:** "Canonical L2 setting" for baseline without tuning, while sequence model is fully trained = asymmetric optimization. Conceded.

**Issue 4:** ROC-AUC is threshold-agnostic; B2B intervention requires precision at the top of the distribution. Precision@10% (secondary) should be primary or co-primary. Conceded.
adjudication_raw:
## Adjudication (Run 3): SaaS Churn Prediction

All four issues conceded. Verdict: critique_wins.
